_meta.description: ChatGPT-like repetition controls for Koda. Prevents identical boilerplate, repeated openers/closers, repeated fallback messages, and copy-paste style answers across turns and regenerations. This does NOT hardcode answers; it enforces variety while preserving factual content.

windows._comment: How far back to look when detecting repeats.

thresholds._comment: Similarity thresholds; runtime computes similarity (e.g., cosine over shingles).

rewriteStrategies._comment: These are strategies (not templates) used by the orchestrator/composer to produce a different surface form with the same meaning.

strategies.description: Swap opener to a different bank variant (or remove opener entirely) while keeping main content unchanged.
Swap/trim conclusion line for a different bank variant (or remove if redundant).
Re-split paragraphs into 1–2 sentence chunks (same meaning) to change surface structure without changing facts.
Reorder bullet list by importance/flow (same bullets, different order), preserving numeric facts.
Light paraphrase with safe synonyms for glue words only (e.g., 'shows'→'indicates'), never changing numbers or named entities.

definitions.notes: If exact duplicate detected, apply a rewriteStrategy (swap opener/closer, rechunk paragraphs, reorder bullets). If regenCount is available, request regeneration with a different variationSeed.
If near duplicate detected, apply safe paraphrase + structure changes; keep all facts identical.
Select a different fallback variant within the same category (anti-repetition) and include a different next-step suggestion.
Swap nav intro microcopy to a different variant (still 1 sentence).
